notes: First pattern week. Sheep debuts (name-exempt). Pattern Tree unveiled — leaf #1. Core: ship, shop, fish, wish.
Chick debuts — fully decodable at debut (ch+i+ck). Core: chin, chop, chat, much.
Highest-frequency digraph — that/this/then/them/with unlock, sentence glue explodes. EAL: th vs s/t minimal-pair drill. Core: this, that, then, with.
ck formalized (known since W21) + FLSZ doubling rule. Biggest single harvest of Level 2 (+40). Core: bell, miss, off, puff.
EAL: Mandarin HAS final ng but it reduces — drill explicitly. Core: sing, ring, king, long.
QUESTION WEEK — gates what/when/where/which/why. when+which decodable; what/where/why enter as heart words. Core: whip, when, whiz, which.

focus: s-blends (st, sp, sn, sm, sw, sc/sk)
notes: Blends = recombination, lighter treatment, picture-vocab heavy. Snake debuts (name-exempt). Core: stop, spin, snap, swim.

focus: l-blends (bl, cl, fl, gl, pl, sl)
r-blends (br, cr, dr, fr, gr, pr, tr)
notes: Initial /r/ NOT hard for Mandarin L1 — normal pace. 'from' unlocks. Core: frog, crab, drum, grin.
EAL HARDEST CATEGORY — zero Mandarin coda clusters. Double repetition, no-schwa modeling. Core: hand, went, jump, nest.
Final /l/ = the ~97% error trap — dedicated minimal-pair material. Core: pink, soft, belt, help.
2nd-biggest unlock of the program. Snake becomes SPELLABLE (name-day). Pattern-card tracing mode debuts. Core: cake, name, gate, snake.
'these' unlocks (th+e_e). Core: cube, mule, tune, these.
Level 2 finale — Pattern Tree left branches full (16 leaves). Book: the cast crosses the bridge into Level 3. Running total 603. Core: ice, cage, catch, bridge.
